review_title: The best sales tool I have seen and used
description: Their automated flows enabled us to reconnect with leads that we thought were lost which helped our business group grow the close rate by over 26%. Best cash we've put into a sales software to date.
author: Jessica W.
pros: I like the lead gen service a ton. We don't work in all states in the US and the general prospects we connect are continually moving so purchasing leads was a misuse of cash as a great many people had left the organization when we reached them. With Amplemarket we don't have this issue, I don't know how frequently they update their database yet we figured out how to make profiles for various areas and after three years we haven’t come up short on leads.
cons: When we began working with Amplemarket the verification of their speculated email adresses was lower and our outreach campaigns used to have high bounce rates. We told their CS team and after a couple of days, our issues were tackled. Aside from this, we have not had any serious issues.

description: Great team who is passionate about what they do, their product and offerings are constantly evolving, one of the best AI products we've worked with.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Their Duo leads functionality uses trigger events to identify prospects who are most likely to respond to an email sequence that Amplemarket's AI already queues up for you. All you have to do is press send.
cons: A bit of a learning curve for new hires being trained on the platform.

description: Overall good experience and the CS team solved my problems really quickly
author: Luciano E.
pros: Intuitive, easy to use, many options to filter data
cons: Bad data quality, cannot rely on the database.

review_title: The most helpful tool in the market
description: Since I'm a founder my time is extremely important. Their smart actions enable me to truly concentrate on my work and just focus on my inbox two or three times each day. I only need to go to my inbox when I receive a slack notification that there is something important, everything else just works on autopilot.
author: Ashley M.
pros: I really like that it spares me time on redundant tasks. Amplemarket helped us refine our sales operation. They helped us create our optimal client profiles and make email campaigns that have great results.
cons: I’ve been a client for over 2 years, there is nothing I truly dislike. Amplemarket's team consistently works superbly and truly tunes in to criticism.

review_title: Amplemarket - The Future of Sales Automation
description: Amazing. If you have unique strategies that you need to scale, Amplemarket can't be beat.
author: James B.
pros: The future of sales is going down the personalization route. This is time intensive and hard to scale. Enter: Amplemarket. They make it easy to send out mass personalized emails. Few things to note: - The first day I started using this software it allowed me to 3X my current outbound strategy. This was so effective that I actually got a public shout out on LinkedIn for the quality of my email. - It is an all in one solution that is perfect for smaller businesses building out there sales process. Sourcing, Contact Info, and Contact Data. There is no other solution that properly combines all of these.
cons: It's an early stage startup so there are some features they are still building. I'm excited to see where will it be in a year. Best part about it is you can actually request features to be built. They also have core functionality (email sending, contact sourcing, and contact data down pat).
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great for outbound sales and automation
author: Rob L.
pros: Easy to use and great AI powered automation - particularly the contact/company search and email validation. Saves a lot of time!
cons: Domain health went down after using, you have to keep a close eye on that.

author: Andres K.
pros: Amazing product and the best customer service I’ve ever seen.
cons: Really nothing. Maybe a few bugs that were fixed in the latest update. Now it’s a machine.
